ddls:
CREATE TABLE "occupations" (
  "occupation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each occupation */,
  "occupation_name" TEXT /* Name of the occupation */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the occupation */,
  "median_income" INTEGER /* Median income for the occupation */,
  "growth_rate" REAL /* Growth rate of the occupation */,
  "is_in_demand" INTEGER /* Indicates if the occupation is in high demand */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time when the record was last updated */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the record */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the record */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("occupation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "income_levels" (
  "income_level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each income level category */,
  "income_category" TEXT /* Income category (<=50K or >50K) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the income level category */,
  "percentage_population" REAL /* Percentage of the population in this income category */,
  "median_age" INTEGER /* Median age of individuals in this income category */,
  "median_education_num" INTEGER /* Median education level of individuals in this income category */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time when the record was last updated */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the record */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the record */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("income_level_id")
)
